metric_value: Chase Manhattan trust operations relied on Univac 96-column card processing; Univac exit from unit-record business in 1972 forced replacement
Chase chose IBM 360/65 mainframe for new trust operations system
Chase janitorial staff inadvertently dumped world's supply of Univac unit-record spare parts at sea over a long weekend
notes: Triggered ADL audit of Chase IT project.

metric_value: Chase IT project produced 400 written COBOL programs with no system flow chart
notes: ADL audit concluded the project would not finish before card platform end-of-life.

metric_value: ADL substituted COSMOS-based approach for floundering Chase IT project, conceived by Murray Sherry
notes: Murray Sherry was Kastner's former PHI boss who hired him into ADL.

metric_value: Kastner was lead designer and chief programmer of the securities subsystem under TOOS
notes: 6000 lines of COSMOS expanded to 25M lines of COBOL; ~6 months parallel design of securities and customer applications.

metric_value: Mary Ellen 'Mel' Holland — Marine Midland alum and first woman with Columbia MBA — co-developed Chase TOOS customer application
Once converted, IBM 360/65 processed 174,000 unique securities held by 25,000+ trust clients with combined value over $10 billion (1975 dollars)
Rockefeller's quarterly Exxon dividends required field width >$99,999,999.99; Kastner asked 'how big' and was told 'one more 9'
notes: Memoir; illustrative of upper-end UHNWI account requirements.

metric_value: Kastner and Holland produced 25M lines of debugged COBOL ready in under a year using COSMOS
Kastner's COSMOS code at Chase reportedly never caused an application rerun once in production
UFRJ teaching hospital administration system designed for Burroughs B6700 university mainframe
Forms carried by bicycle-courier from hospital to data center continually, replacing originally proposed online terminals due to USD foreign-exchange constraints
notes: Social-justice job creation for favela population.

metric_value: Design documents and COBOL code written in English; forms documents translated by professional translators
notes: Burroughs JCL was easier than IBM JCL.

metric_value: Burroughs B6700 indexed sequential access method for COBOL did not work; Kastner wrote a small program to demonstrate the bug, brought results back to Boston for Burroughs fix
notes: Customs incident with magnetic tape at Rio airport.

metric_value: Kastner directed conversion of hospital inventory control system from Burroughs B3500 to B6700 in university environment
Hans Nijkamp was Dutchman hired as UFRJ project leader; Kastner viewed appointment as unnecessary
notes: Memoir tone: critical of project staffing.

metric_value: Peter Kastner's wife Kate (COBOL programmer) joined the UFRJ project; ADL paid two reduced stipends
Barbara Kruesi volunteered from ADLS as third COBOL programmer on UFRJ
Houghton Mifflin was a Honeywell shop running 2000-series mainframe
HM's new order-entry/inventory system was so flawed that bookstores refused to pay invoices and physical inventory failed audits; HM could not close books and risked NYSE suspension
notes: ADL deployed 4-man COBOL team for crisis remediation.

metric_value: After remediation, more than 100 days of HM production had to be re-run for NYSE reporting
HM teenager driving cards/tapes/disks to Honeywell regional data center on Route 128 flipped the van
Bomb threats during HM crisis cleared the building for hours; by the fifteenth, no big deal
Burroughs B1700 with Forte II language and NDL was selected; TC500 intelligent terminals at four locations across country
notes: Five-application: order entry, inventory, shipping, invoicing, sales analysis. Five man-years.

metric_value: Burroughs-spec'd 64KB memory was inadequate; virtual OS paged to slow drum storage causing 20-min compiles
State-of-the-art keyboard-based TC500 terminals with multi-form printing, programmability, cassette storage, 300-bps modem; long-distance phone exceeded $1/min
IBM 370/135 under DOS/VS for salesman incentive bonus system in ANSI COBOL
notes: Tanked transaction edit concept; decision-table per category.

metric_value: IBM 370/158 with CICS and TOTAL database for Nortel North American order processing
notes: Six remote plants/warehouses; order entry, inventory, replenishment, AR, invoicing.

metric_value: DEC payroll/personnel on DEC-10 with online inquiry/update; new hires, terminations, leaves, field changes
notes: Restart/recovery routines; conversational CRT prompting; edit tables.

metric_value: Kastner managed proposal to Citibank for worldwide funds transfer via star-network minicomputers with Federal Reserve, CHIPS, SWIFT, Bankwire and 250 overseas branch interfaces
Kastner contributed lead-analyst study of Parade business systems including central computer plus remote intelligent terminal selection and DP organization
Kastner led team auditing FORTRAN source of pioneering AS&E Computer-Aided Tomography (CAT) product to avoid radiation overexposure
Kastner participated in team writing FORTRAN for US Navy ship-design study (CASDOS)

Kastner brought in on roughly ten ADL design opportunities, absorbing knowledge of Ocean Spray Cranberries, Citibank SWIFT, Wisconsin Electric remote meter reading, Parade production, and Continental Forest Industries kraft paper
notes: Foundational industry breadth that informed later Aberdeen Group analyst work.

metric_value: Wisconsin Electric remote meter reading proposal used AC voltage fluctuation at 5 baud to avoid telephone line connect costs

Kastner's ten-year programmer-analyst career involved a wide breadth of industries, vendors, architectures, and computer technologies, providing unique vantage to look at the computer industry as a whole
notes: Foundation for later Aberdeen Group thought leadership.
